name: RangeSlider

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Configuration of the range slider

- `domain` — the domain you wish to map from.
- `range` — the range you wish to map on to.
- `scale` — a mapping from domain to position
- `unscale` — a mapping from position to domain
- `ticks` — a list of values in domain-space to render on your rangeslider axis.
- `format` — a function to render values from your domain.
- `toMsg` — a way to send the new value of an endpoint to your app as messages.

unions.name: State
unions.comment:
Tracks the drag state, if any, of an endpoint and
    the screenspace x-offset of the rangeslider handle control

values.name: config
values.comment:
A configuration of the range slider allowing you to specify the
type of your domain and the type of scale you wish to use. Note the
type of the range is fixed as `Float` as the scale must be invertable.
values.type: { a | domain : ( domain, domain ), format : domain -> String, onChangeLower : Maybe (domain -> msg), onChangeUpper : Maybe (domain -> msg), scale : domain -> Maybe Float, ticks : List domain, unscale : Float -> Maybe domain } -> Config msg domain

values.name: simpleConfig
values.comment:
A simple configuration for the most common use case of a linear scale over
a floating point domain, with a range starting at 0. This configuration will
automatically calculate some visually `nice` ticks for you given an approximate
number of ticks you want.

You provide the following information in your table configuration:

- `domain` — the lower and upper endpoint of your range.
- `width` — the width of the rangeslider in px.
- `maybeRoundTo` — an optional value to which each endpoint's value should be rounded.
- `maybeTickCount` — an optional approximate tick count. Providing a value of `Nothing` will result in an axis with no tick marks.
- `format` — a function to render values from your domain.
- `toMsg` — a way to send the new value of an endpoint to your app as messages.
values.type: { a | format : Float -> String, maxValue : Float, minValue : Float, onChangeLower : Maybe (Float -> msg), onChangeUpper : Maybe (Float -> msg), step : Maybe Float, tickCount : Maybe Int } -> Config msg Float

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Subscriptions that should be added to your app's own subscriptions.

values.name: update
values.type: Config msg comparableDomain -> Msg -> State -> ( State, Maybe msg )

values.name: view
values.type: StyleConfig -> Config msg Float -> State -> ( Float, Float ) -> Html Msg

values.name: viewUnstyled
values.type: StyleConfig -> Config msg Float -> State -> ( Float, Float ) -> Html Msg
